[2018-07-23 05:30] cactus_wren (cactus): oh man did i miss a pedantic discussion about the definition of AoE??
[2018-07-23 05:31] cactus_wren (cactus): AoE was a term never used in card games before hearthstone; this is because the term comes from warcraft (especially the World Of), which is hearthstone's source material
[2018-07-23 05:32] cactus_wren (cactus): so to get a good definition of AoE in card games, it's best to look at what the term means in warcraft and WoW, which is an effect that affects any unit in a given area, and typically affects all the units in the area the same way
[2018-07-23 05:33] cactus_wren (cactus): so flamestrike in HS is an AoE because it deals 4 damage to all enemy minions; wrath of god in mtg is aoe because it destroys all creatures. if you loosen the definition a bit then stuff like cone of flame is also aoe because it affects 3 creatures
[2018-07-23 05:33] cactus_wren (cactus): someone earlier mentioned tokens as aoe; this is kind of a huge stretch and i don't think making tokens is ever aoe
[2018-07-23 05:33] cactus_wren (cactus): an AoE effect in prismata would probably be like, chill all units or apply 1 damage to all units
[2018-07-23 05:34] _theavatar (The Avatar): Therefore, it’s cost is independent of who it hits, and it can hit a theoretically infinite amount of creatures (assuming an infinite number of creatures can fit in the region)? In short, Cryo Kronus is the only current one.
[2018-07-23 05:34] cactus_wren (cactus): and would likely be incredibly hard to balance, because if it's cost effective it's busto and if it's not cost effective it's useless, so it's only playable in super big wide boards
[2018-07-23 05:34] cactus_wren (cactus): cryo kronus is the one from the campaign final boss?
[2018-07-23 05:34] cactus_wren (cactus): yeah that's for sure aoe
[2018-07-23 05:35] cactus_wren (cactus): and the "area" from "area of effect" is "all your minions"
[2018-07-23 05:35] cactus_wren (cactus): but the only other ways that would work in prismata afaik is like, "freeze all of one type of unit" and that's like
[2018-07-23 05:35] cactus_wren (cactus): that would only exist to punish noobs for not playing around it
[2018-07-23 05:35] cactus_wren (cactus): so the only area i can think of is "all blockers"
[2018-07-23 05:36] _theavatar (The Avatar): Ok we concur. @Terra Reveene was making me question whether the term AoE had evolved in recent years
[2018-07-23 05:36] cactus_wren (cactus): it comes from warcraft
[2018-07-23 05:36] _theavatar (The Avatar): Yep, I used to play WoW
[2018-07-23 05:36] cactus_wren (cactus): hearthstone has changed card game terminology in very odd ways because hearthstone bastardized both warcraft terms and magic terms
[2018-07-23 05:36] cactus_wren (cactus): and it bled over into all the other mainstream games
[2018-07-23 05:37] cactus_wren (cactus): it's like speaking spanish your whole life and then meeting a brazilian
[2018-07-23 05:38] cactus_wren (cactus): but then prismata is also weird because it has lots of its own unique terms, like granularity is, afaik, a completely novel concept to prismata
[2018-07-23 05:38] _theavatar (The Avatar): Well yeah, Prismata ends up being a sort of weird deck builder without luck meets RTS
[2018-07-23 05:39] _theavatar (The Avatar): So it needs new terminology
[2018-07-23 05:39] cactus_wren (cactus): it's magic chess dominion starcraft
[2018-07-23 05:39] _theavatar (The Avatar): Because it’s genre breaking
[2018-07-23 05:39] cactus_wren (cactus): steelspitter is a magic card
[2018-07-23 05:39] cactus_wren (cactus): it has power and toughness and it can attack or block but not both
[2018-07-23 05:40] _theavatar (The Avatar): Haha fair. Chess dominion Starcraft is how I’ve tried to describe it to others, but that only works so well...
[2018-07-23 05:40] cactus_wren (cactus): yeah you forgot the magic
[2018-07-23 05:40] cactus_wren (cactus): i'm serious, magic chess dominion starcraft is like... a very very good summary
[2018-07-23 05:41] _theavatar (The Avatar): Agreed, but imagining that without context is nigh impossible

[2018-07-23 17:21] cactus_wren (cactus): in a normal card game putting aoe in your deck has the implicit cost of "it's bad sometimes even if it's ridiculously efficient when it's good." but in prismata, both players have access to it and you would only ever buy it if it were good, and you would never buy it if it were bad.
[2018-07-23 17:21] cactus_wren (cactus): prismata is interesting like that where you can't really make an overly specific unit because it's just less interesting for gameplay if a unit has one obvious application and no others
[2018-07-23 17:22] cactus_wren (cactus): compare to endotherm kit or vai where it's not always even remotely obvious if they're good, although there are obvious situations where you do/don't get them
[2018-07-23 17:22] cactus_wren (cactus): and fwiw endotherm kit is kinda like aoe

[2018-07-25 15:52] _theavatar (The Avatar): Is there value in clicking thermite core(s) if it does not deny absorb? Or would you rather remove 2/4/6/8 more soak and make them buy back more defense the following turn instead? Obviously threat is much better vs. red than, say, breachproof, but does thermite actually make a difference if you aren't denying absorb?
[2018-07-25 15:54] cachotognax: I guess you can deny soak from doomed units too like plexo
[2018-07-25 15:55] _theavatar (The Avatar): True, although at any appreciable amount of attack they'll just soak on those first...
[2018-07-25 15:56] cachotognax: you need to have a relevant ammout of cores for that to work, but if your opponent has wall for absorb and plexo if you manage to make your attack go below 6 you are gaining value.
[2018-07-25 15:57] medar: that falls under denying absorb
[2018-07-25 15:58] cachotognax: well it might not be that obvious so better to clarify than not clarifying.
[2018-07-25 16:02] _theavatar (The Avatar): I mean you could technically deny soak and not absorb if they had 2 plexos and you cut your damage from 10 to 6, right?
[2018-07-25 16:02] _theavatar (The Avatar): Just @CachoTognax 's example was off

[2018-07-27 14:55] willflame: is it good to buy Blood Phage even if you're not going to use its click ability? (i.e. buying more than 2 with only 1 Animus)
[2018-07-27 14:55] willflame: it seems worth it because it's still a fast Tarsier for 5R + Drone
[2018-07-27 14:57] willflame: and afterward, would you buy a second Animus to power the click ability instead of buying Drones?
[2018-07-27 15:00] _theavatar (The Avatar): If you're buying the animus just to power your bloodphage, then you should have just gone higher econ in the first place. If you're buying the animus to spend at least 1 of its red (like for fangs or tatsu) with the knowledge that you'll be sinking the red later when you have to be buying defense, it's excellent.
[2018-07-27 15:02] _theavatar (The Avatar): And yes, buying a 3rd bloodphage with a single animus isn't necessarily wrong, even though it's fairly uncommon (a 3rd implies econ is still valuable in which case you probably will be buying a second animus anyways, although not always)
[2018-07-27 15:02] shadourow (Rando): Also, bloodphage doesn't need to be clicked to be excellent : That's basically a drone + fast tarsier
[2018-07-27 15:02] shadourow (Rando): And Fast Tarsier, 5R attacker, would be busted
[2018-07-27 15:03] willflame: hmm yeah, that's what i was thinking
[2018-07-27 15:03] willflame: just that in some games where the only offensive red unit is Blood Phage, it feels generally worse to buy Tarsier/Rhino for attack after the first Tarsiers
[2018-07-27 15:04] willflame: so i end up buying all 4
[2018-07-27 15:04] shadourow (Rando): Most of the time, you can't because you need to build a blastforge
[2018-07-27 15:04] shadourow (Rando): then, it is often better to buy double tarsier than Bloodphage + click a bloodphage
[2018-07-27 15:04] willflame: Blood Phage enables early Blastforge though
[2018-07-27 15:05] willflame: really? (double Tarsier > Blood Phage + click)
[2018-07-27 15:05] _theavatar (The Avatar): Yes. If you are going early red then odds are you want to be pushing damage ASAP
[2018-07-27 15:05] shadourow (Rando): I think that TT indeed is better than phage + click blood phage, but didn't actually try to works on it to prove it
[2018-07-27 15:06] _theavatar (The Avatar): Bloodphage+click just isn't aggressive enough and thus gives the higher econ player room to really push attack
[2018-07-27 15:06] willflame: what if the click buys a Steelsplitter/Gauss Cannon instead? then you have faster attack
[2018-07-27 15:07] _theavatar (The Avatar): Hm, so 13 gold 2R and 1B?
[2018-07-27 15:07] _theavatar (The Avatar): Tarsier, Tarsier, Wall is probably the more likely purchase option
[2018-07-27 15:07] _theavatar (The Avatar): But if there's no pressure than Bloodphage+click+Steelsplitter is better
[2018-07-27 15:07] willflame: say 14 gold to allow TTS
[2018-07-27 15:08] willflame: is that better than 1Sclick?
[2018-07-27 15:08] _theavatar (The Avatar): Got to get the sword economy going
[2018-07-27 15:08] shadourow (Rando): Exept if that 1 extra damage matters a lot
[2018-07-27 15:08] willflame: even if this is the second Blood Phage?
[2018-07-27 15:08] _theavatar (The Avatar): ^ If it enables Arka/Bear/breaks the absorb barrier
[2018-07-27 15:09] shadourow (Rando): Why would this be important ?
[2018-07-27 15:09] willflame: because the red could be recycled?
[2018-07-27 15:09] shadourow (Rando): Why would you want that ?
[2018-07-27 15:09] willflame: i guess that's not a huge part of its power
[2018-07-27 15:09] shadourow (Rando): Spend 2 red can be difficult, but it's pretty easy to spend only one each turn, in most of the situations
[2018-07-27 15:09] willflame: sometimes i cycle red to buy Steelsplitters when i have nothing to spend B on
[2018-07-27 15:10] willflame: should i waste the B instead?
[2018-07-27 15:10] _theavatar (The Avatar): If you're spending both red on bloodphage cash, you should have just higher econ
[2018-07-27 15:10] shadourow (Rando): But it feels like you maybe transitioned to blastforge too soon ?
[2018-07-27 15:11] shadourow (Rando): You don't really need to absorb 2 when your opp attack for 2 (rhino OP)
[2018-07-27 15:11] shadourow (Rando): And attack tend to ramp up pretty quickly
[2018-07-27 15:11] willflame: idk, i've been Walling starting from 1 attack most of the time
[2018-07-27 15:11] _theavatar (The Avatar): In no situation should you be wasting tech with 1A and 1B, right? Even Elyot Animus would be 8 drones+1 phage which can go Tariser+click+steelsplitter
[2018-07-27 15:12] shadourow (Rando): I really think you shouldn't @WillFlame that slow down a lot your sword economy
[2018-07-27 15:13] _theavatar (The Avatar): That's almost certainly premature, especially if they're on blue tech with a single steelsplitter
[2018-07-27 15:14] willflame: what if I don't have A for Rhino? like if i went DDB (as p1) against DA , and they buy a Phage
[2018-07-27 15:14] willflame: i feel like i should Wall that 1 damage
[2018-07-27 15:15] _theavatar (The Avatar): Unless you have good reason to go for a meaningfully, you're probably much better off building a splitter
[2018-07-27 15:15] _theavatar (The Avatar): Since the engi takes one damage, then you can buy a second splitter the next turn and defend with your first one
[2018-07-27 15:16] _theavatar (The Avatar): Or just transition into a real attacker and defend with your first splitter
[2018-07-27 15:16] shadourow (Rando): That depends on the situation, but most of the time, I don't feel I need to absorb the first attack if it's only 1 damage and annoy a lot my build order
[2018-07-27 15:17] willflame: hmm, ok. thanks guys!

[2018-07-27 20:54] cactus_wren (cactus): man bombadier is a hell of a unit
[2018-07-27 20:54] cactus_wren (cactus): getting full value off a bombadier is pretty good
[2018-07-27 20:54] cactus_wren (cactus): so what's the deal with arms race
[2018-07-27 20:55] cactus_wren (cactus): it's like very effective at building attack quickly but when both players are just buying all the arms races it's such a difficult position to navigate
[2018-07-27 20:56] ruinedshadows: buy all the arms race is generally bad. The rule of thumb is, if all of the engis you give your opponent with it are getting defense value the very next turn then you shouldn't buy it
[2018-07-27 20:56] ruinedshadows: for this reason usually a max of 3 arms race are bought (cause you're up to 10 damage)
[2018-07-27 20:57] cactus_wren (cactus): i think getting a big absorber is good because it lets you really utilize those engis
[2018-07-27 20:57] ruinedshadows: arms race is a good attack because it is cost efficient but it is not fast and doesn't add pressure (reduces it temporarily)
[2018-07-27 20:57] ruinedshadows: big absorber is generally good
[2018-07-27 20:58] f300xen (f0x): if your opponent isn't buying arms race and you are then it doesn't matter how many you're buying

[2018-07-28 00:22] xyotsuba: @WillFlame sry you got some bad advice here
[2018-07-28 00:23] xyotsuba: Somebody told you to splitter against 1 dmg and then splitter again and hold the first splitter? That’s just nonsense
[2018-07-28 00:23] xyotsuba: First, keep in mind that wall is a good unit and splitter is a bad unit.
[2018-07-28 00:24] xyotsuba: Also keep in mind that the much of the first splitter’s value comes from the ability to hold and absorb which the second splitter can’t do.
[2018-07-28 00:24] f300xen (f0x): paying 1 gold and 1 engi for 1 threat is a shit trade
[2018-07-28 00:25] xyotsuba: My rule of thumb for openings is to get 1 splitter max
[2018-07-28 00:25] xyotsuba: Otherwise forge was too early
[2018-07-28 00:26] xyotsuba: If u splitter first and then they defend with a wall or rhino your splitter has to hold and you end up making another splitter
[2018-07-28 00:27] xyotsuba: (Or wasting blue which is even worse)
[2018-07-28 00:28] xyotsuba: If you wall then you get to splitter next turn and then there is probably something to do with the blue. If there’s nothing to do with the blue at this point the forge was probably bad, unless you are wasting it to buy a key unit like cynestra or lucina)

[2018-07-28 09:52] tiboteebs: I just started playing the campaign, does board position matter?
[2018-07-28 09:57] mqp: nope, it's just for organizational purposes
[2018-07-28 09:58] tiboteebs: from what i see its about resource management without overwhelming your board correct?
[2018-07-28 09:59] tiboteebs: obviously i cant see all the units yet
[2018-07-28 09:59] tiboteebs: im only on the first episode
[2018-07-28 09:59] shadourow (Rando): In front : Defenders and frontline units
Middle : clickable units
Behind : other units

Basically
[2018-07-28 09:59] tiboteebs: i meant the game, less the boardstate however
[2018-07-28 10:00] shadourow (Rando): I'm not sure what you mean about "without overwhelming your board correct" but indeed, you must optimize your resource management, considering the fact that attack is also a resource
[2018-07-28 10:01] tiboteebs: like if i force my board full of units then my opponent can take it out and starve my resources
[2018-07-28 10:01] tiboteebs: so I cant have all my board filled with drones for examp;e
[2018-07-28 10:01] shadourow (Rando): Yeah, because sword economy > gold economy
[2018-07-28 10:01] tiboteebs: yeah thats what i figured
[2018-07-28 10:03] shadourow (Rando): There are many subtilties in the game that you'll discover one and another (such as many exceptions), and some of them are in combat training which is pretty useful to do after the campaign
[2018-07-28 10:03] tiboteebs: I assume you meant that your damage is more important than resources
[2018-07-28 10:04] shadourow (Rando): That is, indeed most of the time the case, but, for example, there are defender with high HP, like [[Energy Matrix]]
[2018-07-28 10:05] shadourow (Rando): 5 HP means that it denies 4 attack each turn
[2018-07-28 10:05] tiboteebs: thats the thing im in on now lol
[2018-07-28 10:05] shadourow (Rando): ahah, ok, maybe I should let you discover the game
[2018-07-28 10:05] tiboteebs: nah dude i asked the question
[2018-07-28 10:07] tiboteebs: i can tell there is an amount of sacrificing board to allow your bigger tech to come online
[2018-07-28 10:08] shadourow (Rando): I'm not sure of what you mean, do you refer to, for example, [[engineer]] you can use to *soak* opp damage ?
[2018-07-28 10:09] tiboteebs: well i mean if you have an overheat ray but you cannot maintain your matrix
[2018-07-28 10:09] tiboteebs: then youre gonna have to sacrifice drones to survive
[2018-07-28 10:10] shadourow (Rando): Indeed, but that mostly the case in the end game, not when you're trying  to get the tech for some legendary impactful unit
[2018-07-28 10:11] tiboteebs: yeah thats what i meant
[2018-07-28 10:12] shadourow (Rando): For that purpose, if this color isn't totally useless otherwise, build a conduit to build [[forcefield]] is great
[2018-07-28 10:13] tiboteebs: yeah i aint there yet lol

[2018-07-28 19:19] simmarine (sim): so how do you effectively use units like urban sentry, xeno guardian, etc? it feels like it can be a bit expensive to get these units sometimes just for an attack, but soaking with them seems a hell of a lot worse. i can understand grabbing one to absorb on, but it doesnt feel very good when i buy more than one
[2018-07-28 19:21] willflame: Xeno Guardian is an cheap way to spend 2 blue if you're developing in red/green
[2018-07-28 19:22] willflame: Urban Sentry is basically a better Steelsplitter if you can spare the green
[2018-07-28 19:22] swapgo: I feel USentry is cheap enough to justify being massed early. Sure it's bad to soak on them but if you're soaking on them you're probably not doing well so it's nice for them to get a last shot before soaking, compared to an S
[2018-07-28 19:22] ruinedshadows: The main use of these units is as "vigilant" defense
[2018-07-28 19:22] ruinedshadows: *main use of massing them
[2018-07-28 19:23] ruinedshadows: they are really good against freeze for that reason
[2018-07-28 19:23] ruinedshadows: you are able to defend the freeze (which won't kill them if they get frozen) while at the same time providing you value by attacking
[2018-07-28 19:24] ruinedshadows: unlike something like a splitter that must be clicked to attack (and then no longer defends the freeze threat)
[2018-07-28 19:24] simmarine (sim): hm vs freeze sounds nice
[2018-07-28 19:24] ruinedshadows: more info here: https://prismatalibrary.blog/2018/01/05/v-defending-against-threat-with-vigilance/
Chill V: Defending Against Threat With Vigilance
This is part 5 of a series on Chill. Parts 1, 2, 3, 4, 6. In part IV, we talked about defending against threat, and how the problem with defending Chill fully is that they will just hold their Chil…
[2018-07-28 19:25] f300xen (f0x): massing urban sentry is usually mediocre since only the first absorbs
[2018-07-28 19:25] amalloy: it's also sometimes fine to soak with them, if you're using that tempo to develop something powerful. mass xeno to protect mass shadowfang

[2018-07-29 00:08] magnars: Someone help me understand Bombarder a little better. I did a blunder in a recent game by clicking a 1-stamina Bombarder, and absorbing on a 0-stamina Bombarder.
[2018-07-29 00:08] magnars: This is a blunder, because even tho I was killing a Wall, that Wall did not have to be re-bought.
[2018-07-29 00:08] amalloy: sounds like not a blunder at all
[2018-07-29 00:08] magnars: So when do you click 1-stamina Bombarders?
[2018-07-29 00:09] magnars: According to the tournament twitch chat it was a big mistake at least.
[2018-07-29 00:09] amalloy: clicking bombarder to kill wall is the same as clicking pixie to kill engineer - ie, it is usually the right thing to do, barring freeze or something. you'd hold it if you can get absorb value, but if you have a 0-stamina one to absorb on that doesn't apply
[2018-07-29 00:10] amalloy: do you have the replay?
[2018-07-29 00:10] magnars: I think the issue is this: If I had used the 0-stamina as soak, I could have spent more on attack - the wall would still be there, but so would the 1 stamina on my absorber.
[2018-07-29 00:10] f300xen (f0x): if you had to buy defense to click the bomb it was definitely a misplay
[2018-07-29 00:11] magnars: the replay is HPRHj-rKEOF
[2018-07-29 00:11] amalloy: that is probably a mistake i've made in every bomber game i've ever played
[2018-07-29 00:12] magnars: yes, it was new information for me, for sure. Which is one of the perks of being caught losing on a tournament twitch stream, I guess!
